title: agrimonia pilosa var. succapitata
intro: Agrimonia pilosa var. succapitata is a plant species in the Rosaceae family, commonly known as a variety of the sticky agrimonia plant, which is native to East Asia. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Agrimonia pilosa var. succapitata include its ability to treat fever, sore throats, and respiratory infections, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, to promote overall health and well-being.
